title: Explain locks in Java. How do they differ from synchronized blocks?

answers.junior: Locks control access to shared resources. Synchronized blocks use implicit locking.
answers.middle: Locks (e.g., ReentrantLock) provide explicit control. Synchronized blocks use implicit monitor locks. Locks offer more features like tryLock().
answers.senior: Locks allow flexible locking, timeout, and interruptible acquisition. Prefer locks for complex scenarios. Synchronized blocks are simpler but less flexible.
answers.tricky: Use locks for advanced concurrency. Analyze lock scope, fairness, and deadlock risk. Prefer java.util.concurrent locks for production systems. Combine with condition variables for signaling.
